answers.answer: A) Availability - during a partition, some nodes must reject requests to maintain consistency. Reason: CAP theorem states you can only have 2 of 3: Consistency, Availability, Partition tolerance. The requirement states: (1) Strong consistency required; (2) Must remain operational during partitions (partition tolerance). With C and P required, must sacrifice A. During a network partition, nodes that can't reach the leader must reject requests to maintain consistency. Option B contradicts the requirement for strong consistency. Option C is impractical (single-node = no fault tolerance). Option D is false - CAP is a fundamental theorem.
